This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_label_warning_resolution.reason: SA Stage B strict tier was rebuilt from deterministic strict-mode engine findings; raw Azure auto-label warnings no longer describe active must_detect labels. Broader statutory coverage remains in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Non-Binding Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Saffron Cloud CJSC (CR 2057789012) by Al-Waha Robotics Holding JSC (CR 1012345670); prospective Tadawul issuer workflows apply; MNPI — not for public release.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 100% of Seller for SAR 420,000,000, payable 60% cash and 40% in new Buyer shares at an implied swap ratio of 1.25:1, subject to CMA approval.

Seller uses biometric time clocks and optional DNA-based asset-tag authentication in R&D labs; such biometric/genetic data will be disabled at closing unless specifically re-authorized by a lawful PDPL basis.

Data localization: customer logs remain in KSA, while non-critical telemetry may be mirrored to EU regions subject to SDAIA transfer permission and standard contractual clauses; retention for acquisition diligence artifacts is 7 years.
Operational footprint: offices in Riyadh Digital City, Jeddah Industrial Valley, and Al-Khobar Tech Park; we will not move the goalposts on headcount guarantees for the first 12 months post-closing.
Closing expected within Q4, subject to completion of vendor due diligence, CMA prospectus waiver where applicable, and Tadawul announcement within the next trading session after signing.

must_detect.matched_text:
Non-Binding Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Saffron Cloud CJSC (CR 2057789012) by Al-Waha Robotics Holding JSC (CR 1012345670); prospective Tadawul issuer workflows apply; MNPI — not for public release.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 100% of Seller for SAR 420,000,000, payable 60% cash and 40% in new Buyer shares at an implied swap ratio of 1.25:1, subject to CMA approval.
Conditions precedent include: (i) CMA M&A no-objection and Tadawul disclosure under Listing Rules; (ii) General Authority for Competition clearance; (iii) Ministry of Commerce commercial register updates; and (iv) ZATCA tax certificates.
This term sheet contains non-public issuer information (MNPI) regarding forecasted robotics margins and cloud recurring revenue; Buyer will maintain an insider list and impose a trading blackout in line with CMA Market Conduct Regulations.
PDPL compliance: parties will process employees’ and contractors’ personal data (including National ID/Iqama numbers for onboarding and CR-linked signatories) under a documented controller-to-controller agreement and conduct a SDAIA Data Transfer Assessment for any cross-border support to the UAE.
Named data handling — Mr. Sami Al-Qahtani, CFO of Al-Waha (Iqama No. 2098765432; email sami.alqahtani@alwaha-robotics.sa), has Type 1 diabetes requiring onsite emergency glucose supplies and is enrolled in facial-recognition access control that stores his faceprint template at the Riyadh plant.
Mr. Sami’s dependent minor, Layan Al-Qahtani (age 15), is included in the group medical plan; her school schedule and insurer member ID will be migrated to the Buyer’s HRIS only after consent, minimization, and encryption at rest.
Seller uses biometric time clocks and optional DNA-based asset-tag authentication in R&D labs; such biometric/genetic data will be disabled at closing unless specifically re-authorized by a lawful PDPL basis.
Religious-practice metadata (e.g., prayer-room booking logs) will not be used for profiling and will be deleted after 30 days; the DEI language in our Oasis DEI Charter 2026 references religion and sexual orientation in a general, non-personal way.
Data localization: customer logs remain in KSA, while non-critical telemetry may be mirrored to EU regions subject to SDAIA transfer permission and standard contractual clauses; retention for acquisition diligence artifacts is 7 years.
Product integration roadmap: align Seller’s “DuneSwitch X” and “SahaCare Edge” with Buyer’s “PalmDrive OS”; codename Project Najd Falcons will be used in communications to avoid leaking intent.
Operational footprint: offices in Riyadh Digital City, Jeddah Industrial Valley, and Al-Khobar Tech Park; we will not move the goalposts on headcount guarantees for the first 12 months post-closing.
Governance: Board seat for Seller’s founder, Dr. Leena Al-Mutairi, for 2 years; customary warranties; dispute resolution via SCCA arbitration in Riyadh; governing law: laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Closing expected within Q4, subject to completion of vendor due diligence, CMA prospectus waiver where applicable, and Tadawul announcement within the next trading session after signing.
